articles.title: Japanese symbols that speak without words
articles.summary: Japanese symbols that speak without words have been used for centuries to convey meaning on signs, flags, and other objects. These symbols often originate from natural sources or geometric shapes and are still widely used today in formal life in Japan. They serve as a form of mutual understanding between the symbol maker and those who see it, transcending language barriers. The use of these symbols is particularly evident in vehicles, where logos have become an integral part of national identity, and in police and fire services, where standardized symbols convey national standards and values.

articles.title: British Columbia, Time Zones, and Postgres
articles.summary: British Columbia's time zone change affects date and time storage in Postgres. The default is to store UTC values, but users consider local time when inputting dates. If stored timestamps are set in the America/Vancouver timezone, they may be off by an hour due to the conversion from UTC to local time during storage and query. To determine if the tzdata package has been updated, use a SQL query that checks for future appointments created before or after the timezone adjustment.

articles.title: Show HN: Oak – Git alternative designed for agents
articles.summary: The Oak alternative Git tool provides a lazy working tree that allows for efficient cloning of repositories without full clone operations, making it suitable for multi-repo agents. Its key feature is its space new agent space, which enables the creation of isolated-worker environments for each agent. The tool also includes several other improvements, such as improved logging and security features like hardened permissions on the SQLite database and fixed issues with branch review and triage.

articles.title: Moebius: 0.2B image inpainting model with 10B-level performance
articles.summary: Moebius is a lightweight image inpainting framework that achieves 10B-level performance with extreme structural compression, pushing the boundaries of industrial foundation models. By introducing Local-λ Mix Interaction (LλMI) blocks and adaptive multi-granularity distillation strategy, Moebius effectively summarizes spatial contexts and global semantic priors into fixed-size linear matrices while preserving complex latent interactions. This results in a >15× acceleration in total inference time compared to the 10B-level industrial generalist FLUX.

articles.title: Nix needs relocatable binaries
articles.summary: Nix needs relocatable binaries is a problem where Nix, or store-based systems, require packages to be installed at specific locations, which can cause issues when using tools like Bazel or Buck2 that rely on sandboxing. This issue arises because the hash calculation for binary substitutions relies on the full store-path prefix, making it difficult to leverage precomputed derivations from binary substituters.

articles.title: How a Computer Should Work
articles.summary: A computer should be an appliance that consumes minimal space, boots quietly with no text or internet connection required, allows users to log in without connecting to the internet, and can run applications offline without saving them. It should also enable users to upgrade systems without erasing data, create user accounts on multiple machines, and support refurbished hardware. By prioritizing simplicity and autonomy, a computer system could be designed to get out of the way and allow developers to focus on creating things quietly and on their own terms.
